taxonomy: Syntherisma aequiglumis is classified within the family Crambidae, which is a family of moths in the order Lepidoptera. The species Syntherisma aequiglumis is a type of moth, specifically a type of grass moth, and is native to North America. Syntherisma aequiglumis belongs to the subfamily Glaphryninae, a subfamily of moths known for their grass-feeding habits. Within the subfamily, Syntherisma aequiglumis is part of the genus Syntherisma, which consists of several species of grass moths. Syntherisma aequiglumis is also known as the brown grass moth due to its brown coloration and grass-feeding habits.
